steps.title: Identify what the blank must mean
steps.content: The passage says corpora can be used for “empirical testing of hypotheses regarding how ______ a word is in spoken and written English.” The example that follows — proving “world” is the eighth most commonly used noun — is about frequency and widespread presence across language. The blank needs a word meaning widespread or prevalent in a measurable sense.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice for fit
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘pervasive’: Means widely present or spread throughout — directly captures the idea that a word appears frequently across spoken and written English, which is exactly what corpus frequency data measures. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘credible’: Means believable or trustworthy; a corpus counts occurrences, not the reliability of a word. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘assertive’: Describes a confident or forceful manner; this is a personality trait, not a property of word frequency. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘profound’: Means deep or meaningful; depth of meaning is not what frequency analysis measures. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
